system.description:
There are things in Tesshari that polite society has agreed, collectively, not to look at directly. The puppet technology of the Hollow Author is the largest and most current of these. Everyone knows puppets exist. Everyone knows the Author uses them. Everyone knows that the technique for creating and operating them came from somewhere — from some tradition or some practitioner who discovered that the Echomind's connection to its physical shell was not as permanent or exclusive as the Iron Sutra suggested. The technique did not appear fully formed in the Author's hands. It was developed. Someone developed it. That development is a history the Sutensai has worked very hard to suppress, and which exists anyway, in fragments, in the practices of those who came to the same discoveries independently.

The Puppet Binder is a player character class because the techniques that make puppets possible are not exclusively the Author's. They can be practiced by individuals — learned, refined, and used in ways that are not the Author's methods, though the structural resemblance is unmistakable. At lower levels, the Puppet Binder works with prepared vessels: bodies that have been made receptive through biological modification, or recently deceased physical forms whose organic-cybernetic integration is still active enough to accept external control. They extend their Echomind into these forms, operating them from a distance, and the sensation is reported as deeply strange by practitioners who attempt to describe it. You are inside something that is not you. You feel what it feels. You act through hands that are not yours. Some practitioners report that the boundary, over time, becomes unclear.

The moral weight of this practice is present in the class text because it must be. The Puppet Binder has chosen to develop an ability that is structurally identical, in its advanced forms, to the technique of Tesshari's most feared and reviled hidden enemy. The differences matter — the distinction between a practitioner who works with constructed vessels or the willing deceased and the Author who subverts the living is real, meaningful, and the entire ethical basis of the class. The differences also narrow as the practitioner advances. The Possessor subclass does not maintain that distinction at its highest levels. The class text does not pretend this is not happening. It is the class of those who have looked at what the power actually is — what it does at its limit — and decided to pursue it anyway, carrying whatever justification for that decision they have managed to build and knowing that the justification will be tested.

Every intelligence service in Tesshari monitors known Puppet Binders. The Sutensai maintains a comprehensive list. Korrath has practitioners in his service and a secondary team watching them. The Hollow Author keeps a close eye on independent Puppet Binders for several reasons, not all of them threatening — an independent practitioner who develops the technique far enough represents both a potential recruit and a potential threat, and the Author wants to know which.
